validation.rule: Each metric must appear in ≥2 distinct locations OR have one high-confidence source (income statement). Material conflicts MUST be flagged.
validation.detail: All five metrics (Revenue, Net Income, EPS Diluted, EPS Basic, Gross Margin) are corroborated across 2–3 distinct sources with zero material conflicts. GAAP figures are consistent throughout. No GAAP vs. non-GAAP discrepancies detected.

confidence.summary: The filing itself is exceptionally clean — GAAP-only EPS (eps_gap: null), 12 corroborations across 6 sources, all 5 high-confidence metrics, and no validation conflicts — pushing the base score up. However, only 1 period of historical data is available (severely limiting cross-source consistency), insider activity is decisively net-negative ($4.45 B net sell value, 6 sells vs. 2 buys), and price-trend data is nearly nonexistent (2 data points, 0 coverage days), preventing meaningful alignment confirmation.

factors.name: Inter-document agreement

All 5 extracted metrics are high-confidence (tally: high=5, med=0, low=0), corroborated across 12 instances from 6 distinct sources within the 10-Q. This factor is as clean as the data schema allows.

No EPS sign reversals or low-confidence period metrics were recorded, but those counts are trivially zero with just one observation. Cross-source consistency cannot be meaningfully assessed; the factor is treated as neutral rather than positive.

factors.name: Insider activity & news
factors.signal: Negative: 6 insider sells vs. 2 buys; net sell value of −$4.45 B over 90 days
factors.detail: Over the 90-day insider window, insiders executed 6 sells against only 2 buys, with a net sell value of −$4,452,766,273.19 — a very large dollar figure suggesting significant conviction among sellers. Recent headlines are broadly macro/sector-level (AI momentum, Fed concerns, Micron, Nvidia, PayPal, SpaceX) with no company-specific positive catalysts identified. The insider sell imbalance is a notable negative signal for data integrity confidence, as it may foreshadow disclosures not yet captured in the single available filing period.
